channels.name: All

cardHistory.track: Basic Understanding of the Risks of Angina #drberg #angina #risks #health

channels.profile: You appreciate intentionality and space — whether in classical restraint or deep, organic groove. Repetition and texture intrigue you, but only when they serve a clear musical purpose. You're not afraid to reject the polished or the chaotic, favoring substance over effect.

currentCard.reason: Purely electronic, minimal, and driving — a complete sonic opposite to the jazz and acoustic territory, probing a high-X, mid-Y, moderately known area.

queue.reason: A sparse, electronic ambient piece that's calm and introspective, existing in a completely different energy and production space from your heard tracks.
Spiritual jazz with harp and drone textures, blending classical Indian influences with free improvisation — calm yet harmonically rich.
Afrofuturist cosmic jazz with Moog synth and spoken word, obscure and sprawling — a polar opposite to your heard clean classical and funk.

channels.name: Angine de Poitrine
channels.profile: You seem drawn to chillwave's hazy, melancholic side—soft synths, gentle beats, and introspective moods. High-energy or dense textures seem less appealing. Dreamy, low-key atmospheres are your sweet spot.

currentCard.reason: A chaotic, free-jazz-influenced explosion of saxophone and feedback, a genuine wild card that still fits their raw aesthetic.

queue.reason: Explores a completely different side of the project—slow, minimal, almost ritualistic ambient-drone with chanted vocals, far from the noise territory you've heard.
Harsh, fractured industrial noise with screamed vocals — a direct hit on your taste for unpolished emotional extremes.

track.name: Qu'est-ce qu'une angine de poitrine et comment en reconnaître les signes ?

queue.reason: A mid-tempo noise collage with manipulated samples and vocal fragments, hitting between your extremes.

channels.name: Atlantic Records

cardHistory.track: Aretha Franklin I Never Loved a Man (The Way I Love You)

channels.profile: You're exploring Atlantic Records' deep catalog with an ear for variety. So far, the soulful Southern R&B of Aretha felt too subdued for you—you might lean toward harder, more driving sounds in rock or syncopated funk. Your taste is still forming, but you're game for either raw electric energy or refined acoustic textures.

currentCard.reason: Cool, calm jazz with classical influences and sparse elegance, acoustic and introspective, moderate in fame—a peaceful counterpoint.

queue.reason: Raw, underground proto-punk from the 1970s, electric and intense but obscure—cult status on Atlantic-distributed label, far from mainstream.
A landmark avant-garde jazz recording on Atlantic, dense and complex with rapid-fire saxophone lines, feeling both cerebral and obscure.
A mellow, doo-wop-infused pop classic on Atlantic, with gentle harmonies and a lazy summer mood that sits calm and mainstream.

channels.name: Bad Bunny
channels.profile: You're giving Bad Bunny's full spectrum a fair shake — from bachata intimacy to industrial dembow. No strong dislikes yet suggests openness, but the real test is which texture feels like home: acoustic warmth, dark club minimalism, or folk storytelling.

track.name: Tití Me Preguntó (Video Oficial) | Un Verano Sin Ti

currentCard.reason: High-energy dembow with trumpet samples sits at a unique intersection of tropical and industrial — probing how you respond to maximalist, dancefloor-driven production.

track.name: BATICANO (Official Video) | nadie sabe lo que va a pasar mañana

queue.reason: A bachata-tinged love song with shimmering guitar and a classic 4/4 sway — probing his softer, more traditional Latin pop side, far from the hyper-modern reggaetón.

track.name: Bad Bunny, Ozuna, Farruko, Arcangel, Ñengo Flow

queue.reason: One of his earliest trap hits with a raw, lo-fi aesthetic and stripped-down beat — testing interest in his underground origins before the global crossover polish.
Hard-hitting trap-infused dembow with aggressive 808s and rapid-fire delivery — pushing toward the high-energy, electronic extreme of his catalog.

channels.name: bembe

cardHistory.artist: MAYKEL BLANCO Y SU SALSA MAYOR

channels.profile: You're drawn to the raw, sacred pulse of bembe—unaccompanied batá drums and Yoruba chants with high spiritual intensity. You reject sterile practice tracks, preferring acoustic ritual music that feels alive and communal.

currentCard.reason: A high-energy mambo track from the father of Afro-Cuban jazz, blending big band brass with driving percussion, mainstream in Latin music circles of the 1950s.

queue.reason: Rumba guaguancó with bembe rhythm pattern, all acoustic percussion and call-and-response, from the most authentic Cuban folkloric group.
This rumba-infused piece blends acoustic guitars with polyrhythmic hand percussion, offering a more melodic and grounded take on bembe traditions.
A rumba-influenced bembe with driving percussion and call-and-response vocals, deeply rooted in Afro-Cuban tradition.

channels.name: Blue Note Records

cardHistory.track: Blues Walk (One night with Blue Note) [HQ]

sessionHistory.track: Blues Walk (One night with Blue Note) [HQ]

channels.profile: You're clearly drawn to Blue Note's golden era—modal and hard bop jazz from the 1960s that's intellectually engaging but never loses its earthy pulse. Acoustic textures with clear melodic structures appeal to you, while free jazz's chaos and electronic elements seem less your path.

currentCard.reason: An avant-garde masterpiece with angular harmonies and free interplay, offering a cerebral and less abrasive alternative to Dolphy's energy, exploring the dissonant edge of Blue Note.

queue.reason: A soulful, funky hard bop groove with a memorable melody and upbeat energy, taking you into a warm, dance-friendly corner of mid-60s Blue Note.
A radical, densely abstract free-jazz album that tests the limits of structure, reaching the most intense and obscure corner of Blue Note's catalog.
Pushing Blue Note's avant-garde edge with angular bass clarinet and free-form structures, this 1964 album offers an intense but controlled exploration of dissonance.

channels.name: Chamber Music

cardHistory.track: C  Franck Piano Quintet in F       Minor, FWV 7        1 mvt
Quatuor Ebène : Bela Bartok  String quartet Nr. 4 C-major Sz 91
YUNDI plays Mozart: Piano Sonata No. 11 in A Major, K. 331 "Alla Turca": III. Rondo alla Turca

cardHistory.artist: Martha Argerich plays Schumann Piano Quintet at the Israel Philharmonic

cardHistory.track: Mozart: Piano Sonata No. 10 in C major, K.330 [Uchida]

sessionHistory.track: Mozart: Piano Sonata No. 10 in C major, K.330 [Uchida]

channels.profile: You gravitate toward Classical and Romantic chamber music with clean, transparent textures and clear melodic lines. String quartets and piano trios feel like your sweet spot—you enjoy moderate emotional expression but steer clear of high-energy or dissonant density. You're still exploring the full range of chamber possibilities.

track.name: ChamberFest Cleveland (2018)
track.artist: BRAHMS: Trio No. 1 in B major, Op. 8

currentCard.reason: A Romantic-era trio blending piano with strings, offering emotional depth and calm intensity, contrasting with the Mozart quartet's Classical clarity.

queue.reason: Early 20th-century French chamber music with impressionist harmonies but still transparent, medium energy (Y=50) and acoustic (X=12), offering a different cultural flavor within Western Europe.

track.name: Verklärte Nacht (Transfigured Night), Op. 4, for string sextet (1899)

queue.reason: Late Romantic/20th-century chamber work with lush chromaticism but still tonal and acoustic (X=8), high emotional intensity (Y=70), and lower mainstream awareness (Z=25), mapping an unmapped high-Y, low-Z territory.

track.name: Claude Debussy: String Quartet in g minor, Op. 10

queue.reason: A shimmering impressionist quartet from the early 20th century that expands your palette with subtle harmonic color while staying within the chamber realm.

channels.timePeriod: Classical era and Romantic era and 20th century classical
channels.notes: chamber music, quartets, trios, piano, Glenn Gould, Vladimir Ashkenazy, Mitsuko Uchida, Vladimir Horowitz

channels.name: Chillwave

cardHistory.track: Feel It All Around (Live on KEXP)

channels.profile: You're gravitating toward warm, mid-tempo chillwave with clean production and mellow grooves. The dislike for the Washed Out track suggests you may want less reverb-soaked haze and more defined rhythm. You seem open to both synthetic and organic textures as long as the energy stays relaxed.

currentCard.reason: Bright and polished chillwave with a sophisticated disco-house influence, maintaining warmth but moving toward a more energetic, driving rhythm section.

queue.reason: Instrumental and lush, with shimmering synths and crisp beats that create a calm yet uplifting atmosphere, exploring the more ambient electronic edge of chillwave.
Lo-fi nostalgia with glitchy analog warmth and a melancholic yet catchy melody—explores a slightly more electronic, beat-driven corner of chillwave.
Spins into a more synth-pop, beat-driven corner of chillwave, testing your tolerance for brighter, busier textures.

channels.name: Cool Jazz
channels.profile: You're drawn to cool jazz's refined textures and unhurried tempos, especially modal vamps and soft brass. West Coast luminaries like Chet Baker and Gerry Mulligan feel like home, but you're open to slightly more structured hard bop as long as it stays elegant. You appreciate melodic clarity and space over density or heat.

currentCard.reason: Modal cool jazz masterpiece from Kind of Blue—spacious, harmonically open, with a serene, introspective mood that extends your piano-forward explorations.

queue.reason: Elegant, classically-inflected cool jazz with vibraphone replacing piano—still harmonically rich but adds a shimmering, less percussive texture.

track.name: Stan Getz & Charlie Byrd - Samba Triste.

queue.reason: Late-60s bossa-tinged cool jazz from Stan Getz—smooth, melodic tenor sax with gentle guitar, blending South American rhythm into your cool aesthetic.
A foggy, baritone sax-led ballad from the 1963 album of the same name, exploring a deeper, more nocturnal sonic territory far from the bright modal harmonies you've heard.
queue.category: Jazz > Cool Jazz > West Coast Jazz

channels.name: CTI Records
channels.profile: You're drawn to the sophisticated, warm fusion sound of early-70s CTI—crisp electric pianos, punchy horns, and funky basslines. You enjoy both driving grooves and reflective, textural moments, always with a polished yet soulful edge.

currentCard.reason: A funky jazz-fusion reimagining of the Strauss classic with electric piano and full CTI production, bright and energetic.

track.name: Uptown Conversation - from Uptown Conversation by Ron Carter

queue.reason: Chamber jazz with an intimate, sparse bass-led setting, cool and understated.
Lush, vibraphone-driven modal jazz with a soulful, meditative feel, showcasing CTI's refined side.
Smooth, bluesy tenor sax with a funky backbeat and warm organ, capturing CTI's accessible yet sophisticated side.

channels.name: Deconstructed Club
channels.profile: You're drawn to deconstructed club's extremes—either hyper-digital overload or stark, empty soundscapes. Texture and atmosphere matter more than steady beats, and you're unafraid of abrasive, unconventional choices.

currentCard.reason: A drone-based deconstructed club piece with calm, sparse instrumentation and meditative quality, exploring low-energy calm territory.

queue.reason: A glitchy, abstract deconstructed club track with a moderate energy level and underground appeal, balancing experimental electronics with rhythmic structure.
An intensely chaotic and maximalist deconstructed club piece built from distorted synth shards and breakneck percussion, exploring a high-energy, fully electronic zone.

track.name: Victor 20 1727 B – Just A Little Fond Affection – Charlie Spivak and his Orchestra

queue.reason: Vast, cavernous sound design with only hints of rhythm—an ambient-leaning deconstructed piece that explores stillness and empty space, far from the intensity of Nkisi.

channels.notes: Deconstructed Club — dance music taken apart and reassembled wrong, on purpose. Steady kicks replaced with metallic percussion, glass-shattering samples, and rhythms that vanish mid-bar. It merges global rhythms like trap, ballroom, and grime with an abrasive post-industrial sound, and it's generating a steady stream of highly-rated releases through 2025 and into 2026. Think: music for a club that's actively collapsing.

channels.name: Deep House
channels.profile: You have a clear affinity for deep house with jazzy textures—smooth piano chords, warm bass, and intimate vocals. Your taste leans mid-tempo and polished, favoring artists like Kaskade and Llorca from the late 90s to mid-2000s. You appreciate subtle groove over intensity.

track.name: DEPECHE MODE REMIX | DEEP HOUSE MIX | RELAXING / CHILL OUT MUSIC | NIGHT DRIVE

currentCard.reason: Soulful vocal chops over a rolling bassline and soft Rhodes chords, capturing the introspective mood you lean into.

track.name: Deep House Vibes 2026 | Tropical House Journey
track.artist: Best Deep House Songs Of All Time

queue.reason: An ambient-tinged deep house piece with slow, evolving textures and almost no beat, testing the boundary of what still qualifies as dance music.

track.name: Deep House Mix 2026 🖤 Just Relax & Sexy Night Drive | Smoke Mood (Nu Disco & Vocal House)

queue.reason: Smooth, downtempo deep house with lush pads and a soft vocal sample, exploring a more ambient-leaning region with low electronic density.
Driving, slightly more percussive deep house with analog synth lines, probing a moderate energy region with a tech-house edge.

channels.notes: Deep House, Kaskade, Daft Punk, Andy Caldwell, Blue Six, Llorca, Mark Farina

channels.name: ECM label
channels.profile: You gravitate toward ECM's core: acoustic purity with space and atmosphere, from solo piano to sax-driven meditations. You value the label's contemplative depth and avoid mainstream pop, preferring its underground edges and fusion hints.

track.name: Keith Jarrett | The Köln Concert | Full Album (1975)

currentCard.reason: Solo piano improvisation from ECM's most iconic live recording, blending calm introspection with spontaneous intensity at moderate energy.

queue.reason: Minimalist composition with pulsing acoustic instruments, dense yet calm, evolving slowly — ECM's contemporary classical anchor.

track.name: Arvo Pärt "Fratres" (original version, 1977) performed by Hortus Musicus (1979)

queue.reason: Intense, sacred minimalism with strings and percussion, deeply emotional and obscure — a spiritual high point far from mainstream pop.
An electric ECM track with distorted guitar and driving fusion energy, pushing toward intensity while retaining the label's airy production.

channels.name: ESP-Disk

cardHistory.track: Choral Scholars of University College Dublin

channels.profile: You're diving deep into ESP-Disk's rawest corners — free jazz screech, lo-fi folk whispers, and garage punk chaos. Spiritual intensity and amateur authenticity thrill you more than polish. You value artistic fearlessness above all.

currentCard.reason: A free jazz masterpiece from ESP-Disk, this track is intense, spiritual, and deeply avant-garde — pushes the energy and rawness far beyond mainstream jazz, exploring high-intensity experimental sound.

queue.reason: Psychedelic folk with lo-fi production and poetic strangeness — acoustic but eerie, a different corner of ESP-Disk's outsider world.
Shrieking, ecstatic free jazz with collective improvisation — the spiritual and chaotic energy aligns with your profile for fearless artistic expression.
A sprawling, cosmic free-jazz suite from ESP-Disk — intensely spiritual, dense, and far from mainstream, probing a high-energy, underground electronic-acoustic blend.

channels.name: Folk Horror Hauntology
channels.profile: You're drawn to slow, immersive drift where acoustic and electronic borders dissolve. Fourth world textures and minimalist repetition feel natural to you, and you prefer the obscure edge of these scenes — artists few have heard of but who build entire worlds in a single tone.

track.name: Everywhere at the end of time (stage one) - full album (2016)

currentCard.reason: Hauntological ambient exploring memory and decay through manipulated ballroom samples

queue.reason: Unyielding acid folk with medieval modes, flutes, and cacophonous vocals embodying folk horror madness
An eerie electronic piece with glitchy textures and disembodied vocals, evoking ghostly broadcasts and digital decay from a hauntological perspective.
A dark, ritualistic folk chant with apocalyptic lyrics and sparse instrumentation, evoking the uncanny and the esoteric traditions of folk horror.

channels.notes: Folk Horror Hauntology — the sonic equivalent of a cursed children's TV show from 1973. It's a close cousin of hauntology — the cultural movement evoking unsettling nostalgia for lost futures — and blends neoclassical instrumentation with spooked, ghostly production applied to old folk standards. Getting real critical traction as an identifiable scene.

channels.name: Fourth World Minimalist Drone
channels.profile: You gravitate toward slow, ritualistic drone and minimalist textures that blend acoustic and electronic elements. Obscure spiritual recordings appeal more than mainstream ambient, and you value subtle evolution over dramatic shifts.

track.name: " Last Night The Moon Came" Jon Hassell

currentCard.reason: A fourth world dreamscape where trumpet and electronic drones merge, perfectly balancing acoustic warmth with synthetic haze at a low energy.

queue.reason: Looping tape decay produces a hypnotic, melancholy drone that feels both ancient and digitally fractured, far from mainstream.

track.name: the well tuned piano : la monte young, marian zazeela

queue.reason: A monumental, just-intonation piano drone piece that pushes minimalism into a dense, contemplative ritual, highly obscure.
Harold Budd's minimalist piano and Eno's generative treatments create a serene, spacious soundscape—cult, calm, and distinctly fourth world.

channels.notes: Fourth World Minimalist Drone — a collision of non-Western ritual textures with American minimalist composition. Bandcamp is actively noticing artists cobbling together fourth world music, drone, and minimalism into intoxicating instrumental records right now, which is a reliable leading indicator of a scene forming.

channels.name: Funk

cardHistory.track: James Brown Get Up (I Feel Like Being a)
Sly & The Family Stone ~ Family Affair 1971 Disco Purrfection Version

sessionHistory.track: Sly & The Family Stone ~ Family Affair 1971 Disco Purrfection Version

channels.profile: You're gravitating toward funk's sultrier side — sparse, bass-forward arrangements with room to breathe, often laced with cinematic or romantic undertones. Dense brass or frantic percussion haven't clicked; instead, cool synth textures and soulful restraint seem to be your lane.

track.name: Sign O' The Times (Official Music Video)

currentCard.reason: Minimalist synth-driven funk with dark, cinematic lyrics and a spare drum machine beat — exploring a more electronic and somber side of Prince's range.

queue.reason: High-energy, horn-driven funk with a punchy bass line and a mainstream pop-funk sheen, testing your tolerance for polished production after the raw James Brown tracks.
A shimmering, synth-laced funk track with a cool, calm energy that explores the minimalist synth textures you liked while staying ultra-smooth.
A deep, bass-driven groove with a laid-back, romantic feel—minimal brass, spacey atmospheres, far from dense funk.

channels.name: Gothic Americana

cardHistory.track: Handsome Family  • Far From Any Road (Main Title Theme from True Detective) Official Lyric Video

channels.profile: You're drawn to the stark, minor-key heart of gothic Americana — raw voices, fiddle, and upright bass over any gloss. Murder ballads and preacher's fire resonate; you'd follow David Eugene Edwards into almost any shadow.

currentCard.reason: A stark, minimalist story-song with fingerpicked acoustic guitar and a low, weathered voice, evoking dusty highways and violence.

queue.reason: A literary, narrative-driven indie-folk track with a slow build and ominous strings, detailing a murder from the killer's perspective.

track.name: In Hell I'll Be In Good Company [Official Music Video]

queue.reason: An upbeat yet macabre bluegrass stomp with fiddle and banjo, delivering a murder ballad feel through high energy and darkly comic lyrics.
A stark, minor-key murder ballad with only two voices and a single guitar — embodying the sparse, haunted end of gothic folk without a shred of polish.

channels.notes:
Country → Gothic Americana
Also called country noir — murder ballads, minor keys, fiddle and upright bass, lyrics about sin and redemption. Where Johnny Cash's shadow lives. The dark, literary, morally ambiguous cousin that Nashville pretends doesn't exist.

channels.name: Hamza El Din
channels.profile: You're drawn to acoustic, hand-crafted sounds from Africa and the Middle East—Nubian oud, Malian guitar, hypnotic repetition. Sparse textures and meditative calm resonate, but you're also curious about raw, organic intensity as long as it stays clear of synthetic production.

currentCard.reason: Hypnotic oud and tar with a meditative pulse from Nubia, perfectly matching your acoustic and sparse leanings.

queue.reason: Electric guitar desert blues from the Sahara—intense yet deeply hypnotic, marking a new territory with gritty intensity.
A transcendent qawwali with hypnotic vocal repetition and sparse percussion, blending devotional intensity with acoustic purity.

channels.name: Hard Bop

cardHistory.track: Blue Bossa I Kenny Dorham I Jazz

channels.profile: You're drawn to classic Blue Note hard bop with a love for soulful, melodic lines and a strong rhythmic backbone. You appreciate both the driving, intense energy of a live-wire session and the more relaxed, blues-infused grooves, showing a versatile ear within the genre.

currentCard.reason: A funky, soulful fusion of hard bop and early jazz-funk with a fat bass line and open, spacious feel — stretches toward the electric side of the Blue Note sound.

queue.reason: a long-form, modal-tinged hard bop workout with a mellow but steadily building groove — less known than the famous hits, exploring a more hypnotic pocket.
An earthy, slightly more obscure hard bop session with a bluesy, soulful vibe that skips the polished coolness you disliked — it's grounded and warm.
A live recording by the composer of the hard bop classic 'Moanin'', this version is rawer and less polished than studio takes — darker vibe, more immediate energy.

channels.name: Hip Hop
channels.profile: You're drawn to hip-hop with lyrical depth and narrative heft, especially 90s boom-bap and soulful East Coast styles. You appreciate warm, sample-based production and emotionally charged storytelling, but you avoid minimal or overly synth-heavy beats. You're open to modern complexity as long as the instrumental richness and vocal presence remain front and center.

currentCard.reason: A foundational hip-hop track with a warm soul sample and narrative rags-to-riches storytelling, fitting your taste for lyrical depth and piano loops.

queue.reason: A fierce, politically charged 2015 track with dense production and raw anger, showcasing modern lyrical complexity and a driving beat.
A gritty, jazz-sampled 1994 track with vivid street narrative and laid-back yet urgent flow, defining East Coast boom-bap at its peak.
queue.category: Hip-Hop > East Coast Hip-Hop > Boom Bap

queue.reason: Hard-hitting, minimalist trap-influenced beat with Kendrick's commanding delivery—exploring a more electronic, modern, and chart-dominant corner of the space.

channels.notes: Jay-Z, Tupac Shakur, Nas, Eminem, The Notorious B.I.G., Rakim, and Kendrick Lamar

channels.name: Hyperpop
channels.profile: You dive headfirst into hyperpop's most chaotic extremes—distorted vocals, glitchy production, and aggressive electronic textures. Your taste rewards maximalist noise but shuns anything too polished or mainstream.

currentCard.reason: A dreamy, pitch-shifted hyperpop ballad with glossy production and emotionally distant vocals, exploring the genre's softer side.

queue.reason: A sprawling, maximalist hyperpop track with unexpected tempo shifts and glitchy production, embodying the genre's absurdity.
Sophie's sleek synthpop-meets-EDM with a twist of surrealism — polished yet alien, a different texture of hyperpop.
Kero Kero Bonito's jittery, video-game-inspired pop with detuned vocals and lo-fi charm — dreamier, less aggressive.

channels.notes:
Pop → Hyperpop
The genre that sounds like pop if pop were designed by a malfunctioning robot. Distorted vocals, heavily processed synths, aggressive beats, and elements of electronic, pop-punk, and noise music — it challenges traditional genre conventions entirely. Think pop taken to a place of deliberate absurdity.

channels.name: John Coltrane
channels.profile: You're starting with a quiet, introspective Miles Davis (Autumn Leaves) but seem open to his more adventurous electric side. No strong likes yet — this session will test how much density, funk, and orchestral drama you enjoy.

currentCard.reason: A landmark spiritual suite that balances hypnotic repetition with ecstatic free soloing, mapping to high intensity yet accessible spiritual jazz.

queue.reason: An Eastern-tinged modal epic built on a two-chord vamp, blending lyricism with hypnotic soprano saxophone exploration.
A hard-bop classic with bluesy swagger and crisp ensemble interplay, representing his accessible, mainstream side.
A haunting, slow-burning ballad from 1963, with Coltrane's tenor sax delivering a solemn, spiritual cry over a sparse backdrop—far from the fire of 'Impressions'.

channels.name: Lo-fi
channels.profile: You gravitate toward lo-fi's warm, nostalgic side — soft piano loops, vinyl crackle, and relaxed tempos. The introspective mood draws you in, while higher-energy beats or dense electronic textures seem less appealing for now.

track.name: Best of lofi 2018 🎶 beats to chill/study to

currentCard.reason: A quintessential lo-fi stream that sets the calm, nostalgic tone of the genre with its warm, vinyl-crackle beats.

queue.reason: A simple, nostalgic lo-fi beat with gentle piano and vinyl noise, representing the ultra-minimalist, bedtime-vibes subgenre.
A lo-fi pop-jazz track with a bright, upbeat grove, using crisp samples and a modern mellow delivery, expanding lo-fi beyond just beats.

track.name: Year One [3 Hour Chiptune, Fakebit, Video Game Music, etc. Compilation]

queue.reason: Lo-fi with 8-bit synth textures and a calm, rainy-day atmosphere — shifts toward retro electronic without leaving the genre.

channels.name: microtonal math rock

cardHistory.track: Tina Turner Passes Away At 83, Cops Check On Ja Morant After Mysterious Instagram Post +More
It Tastes Too Good to be This Healthy! ~ Golden/Turmeric Milk Recipe
السلسلة الكوميدية زوند زوند | الحلقة الرابعة | Zond Zond Ep 4
THEY KEEP SILENCE 그들은 말이 없다

channels.profile: You're drawn to shadowy, slow-burning jazz and neoclassical textures, with a preference for sparse, moody atmospheres over high energy. This microtonal math rock exploration introduces precise, rhythmically complex instrumentals that retain a sinister, introspective edge—potentially expanding your taste into technically demanding, unconventional rock territories.

currentCard.reason: French math rock quartet known for interlocking guitar lines and irregular time signatures, though standard tuning — a bridge from your disliked intense territory to a more melodic but still precise zone.

queue.reason: Though more sample-based, this US artist uses microtonal textures and shifting time signatures with dense, multi-instrumental compositions that push boundary of your constraint.

track.name: Clarinet: What people THINK is hard vs what is actually hard #clarinet

queue.reason: Wild card: a US/Canada duo using custom 19-tone guitars and triggered electronic loops, creating dissonant yet danceable polyrhythms at a higher energy (80, 85) — far from any heard space.
Polish cellist using prepared cello and electronics in a hypnotic, sparse math-rock framework — offers a more introspective, minimal take on microtonal friction.

channels.notes: microtonal math rock -- Microtonal or unconventional tuning systems applied to high-precision rhythmic rock. Two to four musicians, minimal or no vocals, interlocking instrumental parts with shifting or irregular time signatures. Unsettling harmonic friction from notes outside standard Western scales. Dense but not chaotic — technically demanding, compositionally deliberate. Influenced by math rock, progressive rock, and avant-garde composition but not strictly any of them. May include live looping. Costumed, anonymous, or visually theatrical acts welcome but not required. Quebec, European, or international underground.

channels.name: Miles Davis
channels.profile: You're exploring Miles Davis across his entire stylistic range — from the featherlight touch of 'All Blues' to the roaring electricity of 'Pharaoh's Dance'. You're open to both acoustic simplicity and dense, polyrhythmic experimentation, favoring cool, spacious moods but not shying from intensity.

track.name: In A Silent Way ( Full Album )

currentCard.reason: The title track from '69 — a hypnotic, ambient-electric meditation with organ and gentle guitar.

track.name: Ascenseur pour l'échafaud - Lift to the Gallows (Full Album)

queue.reason: Late-50s noir jazz recorded live with French session musicians — far from your electric explorations in its acoustic intimacy and European cool.
Early 90s hip-hop/jazz hybrid — brings in drum machines and rap samples, a short-lived but genuinely different electric branch you haven't heard.

track.name: Miles Davis and Friends in Paris 1991, Title: Jean-Pierre

queue.reason: Mid-80s funk-fusion with layered bass, percussion, and soprano sax — intense, dense, and relatively obscure. High electronic (x≈75), high energy (y≈80), low exposure (z≈35).

channels.name: Mongo Santamaria
channels.profile: You're locked onto Mongo Santamaria's conga-driven Latin jazz, craving tight, energetic grooves that keep moving. So far you've shied away from slower tunes, suggesting a preference for rhythmic intensity over calm introspection.

track.name: Canta bajo (Mongo Santamaria introduces La Lupe)

currentCard.reason: A slow, meditative bolero with delicate guitar and restrained vocals, offering a calm, introspective contrast to your previous picks.

queue.reason: A soulful, mid-tempo Afro-Cuban classic with conga-led rhythm and a modal feel, fitting the earthy pulse you enjoy while keeping energy moderate.
A funky, boogaloo-infused track with electric piano and driving horns, bringing a slightly more electronic and mainstream energy to the session.
A folkloric adaptation of the traditional son jarocho with acoustic guitars and festive percussion, rooted in rural tradition and low energy.

channels.name: Movie Soundtrack

cardHistory.track: John Williams & Wiener Philharmoniker – "Main Title" from "Star Wars: A New Hope"

cardHistory.artist: 영화 언어의 정원(The Garden of Words)

cardHistory.track: Gladiator • Now We Are Free • Hans Zimmer & Lisa Gerrard
Solaris Soundtrack Track 4  “Will She Come Back?” Cliff Martinez
Morricone conducts Morricone: The Mission (Gabriel's Oboe)

channels.profile: Your taste leans toward film scores with strong, identifiable melodies and a clear emotional narrative, favoring orchestral or synth-driven sounds that build naturally over ambient or jarring textures. You enjoy high-energy moments when they feel earned by the score's progression.

currentCard.reason: A fun, upbeat pop-rock anthem from a comedy soundtrack, offering a catchy and playful energy with synths and guitars in a different sensory area, very mainstream and accessible.

queue.reason: Grand, sweeping orchestral melody from a blockbuster — energetic but not harsh, perfectly narrative and widely known, contrasting the previous sparse western.
A lush, acoustic orchestral piece with a gentle, emotional melody and spacious arrangement, exploring a calm, traditional area.

track.name: Vertigo: Prelude/The Nightmare, Scene d'Amour - Stéphane Denève | Made in America

queue.reason: A moody, orchestral piece with swirling strings and a haunting melody, underground but narrative-driven and acoustic.

channels.name: Neoclassical Dark Jazz
channels.profile: You're drawn to ritualistic, slow-burning textures that abandon conventional song forms for atmospheric weight. Dark piano, muted brass, and drone-like repetition resonate with you, especially when the mood is cinematic and obscure. Neoclassical dark jazz and its brooding, minimal cousin dark jazz align well with your taste for the ceremonial and the underground.

track.name: I Forsee The Dark Ahead, If I Stay (Full Live Album)

currentCard.reason: Dark jazz with slow-burning noir atmosphere and electronic textures; sits at a moderate acoustic/electronic midpoint with introspective intensity.

track.name: Do You Know The Mystery Of UVB-76? #shorts

queue.reason: Dark ambient jazz with field recordings and sparse piano—meditative and deeply introspective.
Cryptic, spoken-word neoclassical dark jazz with minimal piano and eerie silence, opening an unmapped region of lyrical abstraction.

track.name: 【MULTI SUB】一口气看完《仙子慢点走：另类废柴》1-2季：开局摆摊卖破烂！结果一句话直接捅出大麻烦，整个修仙界都炸了！

queue.reason: Cello and processed drone textures, blending neoclassical minimalism with dark ambient — far from any prior suggestion, testing your tolerance for abstract instrumental darkness.

channels.notes: Neoclassical Dark Jazz — a growing movement blurring jazz-adjacent composition, neoclassical writing, and progressive rock atmospheres into dark, piano-driven statements — the kind of music that sounds like it belongs in a candlelit library during a moral crisis. Critics are actively naming it as a scene in 2025–26, not just a description.

channels.name: Neofolk Dark Ritual

cardHistory.category: Neofolk Dark Ritual > American Occult Folk

channels.profile: You're gravitating toward slow, darkly ceremonial neofolk built from ancient acoustic instruments — hurdy-gurdy, frame drums, nyckelharpa. The ritualistic, meditative atmosphere and underground cult appeal strongly, while electronic or high-energy sounds don't seem to fit your mood. You prefer immersive, pagan-inspired worlds over polished performances.

currentCard.reason: Deep, droning drone with tagelharpa and heavy frame drum, moving into more driving, processional energy while maintaining total acoustic ritual character.

track.name: Darkness At The Heart Of My Love (Official Audio)

queue.reason: Sparse acoustic guitar and brooding vocals with ritualistic restraint — introspective, melancholic neofolk that fills the calm, intimate corner of the space.
Galician neofolk with hurdy-gurdy and sparse acoustic arrangements evoking a dark, ceremonial forest atmosphere — highly obscure and fitting your preference for minimal, introspective ritual music.

track.name: Europe in the Wake of the Fall of Rome | The Dark Ages | Full Special | History

queue.reason: Explores unmapped territory with melancholic neofolk that adds subtle post-punk and martial elements, over 40 units from all heard tracks.

channels.notes: Neofolk Dark Ritual — acoustic folk instruments (hurdy-gurdy, frame drums, nyckelharpa) arranged to feel like a ceremony rather than a performance. New releases are arriving continuously through early 2026, with a dedicated and growing underground following that spans goth, pagan, and neoclassical adjacent scenes.

channels.name: Phonk

cardHistory.track: THE MOST STREAMED PHONK SONGS OF ALL TIME
Gotham S2E1 🤡💃 Jerome Tries to Charm Barbara but Gets Rejected

channels.profile: You're gravitating toward the darker, bass-heavy side of phonk, craving distorted kicks and that hypnotic cowbell. High-BPM tracks energize you, though slower atmospheric loops with a Memphis edge still work. Avoid the overplayed mainstream anthems.

currentCard.reason: An experimental piece where heavy bass distortion and rapid-fire vocal chops push the boundaries of phonk toward industrial and noise territory.

queue.reason: This track blends high-BPM energy with eerie synths and cowbells, offering a more aggressive drift variant that explores new intensity territory.
This track focuses on the iconic cowbell and Memphis rap influences, with a steady groove and chopped vocals that align with phonk's roots.

track.name: This is What Quavo did at Takeoff's Funeral.🥺😭♥️😔

queue.reason: A classic Memphis-rap infused phonk track with lo-fi production, eerie samples, and a slower, more hypnotic vibe that explores the genre's atmospheric and underground side far from the mainstream intensity.

channels.notes:
Hip-Hop → Phonk
Mixing old-school beats with new-school stories, phonk is dark, Memphis rap-influenced, chopped and screwed, with an eerie cowbell-heavy aesthetic. Enormous on TikTok and gym playlists simultaneously — a strange combination.

channels.name: Primus
channels.profile: You gravitate toward Primus's weirder, more textured corners, shying from their biggest hits in favor of tracks that showcase fluid dynamics and storytelling. The slap-bass technicality and off-kilter rhythms are clearly your draw, but you're also open to their quieter, more atmospheric moments.

track.name: Jerry Was a Race Car Driver (Live at MTV's Haunted House Party) (1993) (1990s Les Claypool)

currentCard.reason: An up-tempo, virtuosic track with a chaotic carnival-like energy, showcasing Claypool's technical prowess.

queue.reason: A sprawling, cinematic narrative with eerie slide bass and a slow-burn horror atmosphere — maps to the calmer, more theatrical side of your taste.
A lumbering, carnivalesque prog epic with a clarinet solo and a drunken groove — probes the region where Primus gets bizarrely orchestral.

track.name: Professor Nutbutter's House of Treats, Washington, DC 8/14/2024

queue.reason: A carnivalesque, almost childlike melody played on bass and xylophone that quickly warps into eerie, chaotic territory — a darker, more experimental Primus.

channels.name: Rihanna
channels.profile: You're enjoying Rihanna's range, from ballads to dance bangers, without rejecting anything yet. Keep an open ear—your taste might lean toward her most soulful or unexpected moments.

currentCard.reason: Soulful ballad with vintage R&B doo-wop influences and powerhouse vocals, showcasing a lush, emotionally intense side.

track.name: Pon de Replay Official Music Video

queue.reason: Early dancehall-influenced debut single with sparse beat and Caribbean flavor, lower energy but still catchy and grounded in her origins.
Minimal piano-and-vocal ballad — calm and intimate, probing the sparsely acoustic side of her catalog.
A reggae-influenced pop track with a slower, acoustic-driven groove and raw storytelling, contrasting the electronic pop sound.

channels.name: Riverside Records

cardHistory.track: Thelonious Monk || The Complete Riverside Recordings Vinyl Box
New Arrivals & Killer! Audiophile Restocks + LP Review Talks John Coltrane, Robert Glasper

channels.profile: You've got a deep affinity for acoustic, small-ensemble jazz from the late 50s and early 60s, especially the Riverside catalog. You lean toward reflective, unhurried textures but aren't afraid of bluesy drive when the interplay is genuine.

currentCard.reason: A 1963 avant-garde jazz album blending African rhythms with free-form solos, intense and percussive, but obscure even among Riverside's catalog, pushing the underground boundary.

queue.reason: A 1958 hard bop masterpiece with driving bluesy energy, widely known within jazz circles but still niche to the general public, hitting a moderately mainstream spot.
Hard-bop with a driving, energetic blues edge—higher energy than typical ballads but still firmly acoustic and small-ensemble classic.
Hard-bop classic from the Riverside family that brings a bluesy, energetic edge, testing your tolerance for a more driving horn focus.

channels.name: Sade
channels.profile: You're drawn to Sade's refined, sultry soul — warm brass, silky vocals, and unhurried tempos. Your taste leans toward polished 80s/90s adult R&B with emotional depth but no rush. You appreciate elegance, space, and a timeless, sensual mood.

currentCard.reason: The epitome of Sade's cool jazz-pop — silky sax, crisp production, and an unhurried, nocturnal groove that sits perfectly in your calm elegance lane.

queue.reason: A stripped-down, gospel-infused ballad featuring only piano and voice — the most minimal, intimate, and raw version of Sade's signature calm, with almost no production sheen.

track.name: Nothing Can Come Between Us (Live) [Widescreen Music Video]

queue.reason: A mid-tempo track with punchy horns and a bright, confident swing — Sade's most 'pop-band' moment, still sophisticated but with a sharper, more energetic edge.
A slightly more intense, synth-laden atmosphere with a steady beat — expands your territory into early-90s polished trip-hop-influenced soul.

channels.profile: You prize clarity and character in Baroque keyboard works, particularly Scarlatti. You respond to performances with clean articulation and moderate fire, rejecting both blandness and over-ornamentation. Harpsichord and piano both work when the touch is purposeful.

track.name: Pletnev. Nutcracker.
track.artist: Kanke Oleksii. Scarlatti Sonata in F minor K.466.  P. Tchaikovsky

currentCard.reason: A stormy, intensely passionate minor-key sonata at maximal energy, checking if the Horowitz recording's rejection was about performance style or the key itself.

track.name: Sonata in G minor K450 (Ivo Pogorelich)

queue.reason: A compact, dark minor-key sonata with dramatic harmonic twists at moderate energy, testing whether the distaste for minor keys needs high intensity or not.

track.name: Scarlatti Sonata in A major K. 322

queue.reason: Elegant and serene with a flowing, almost improvisatory character — a sunlit major-key antithesis to heavy-handed readings.
A famously demonic, fugal sonata far from anything heard — dark, weighty, and structurally radical on harpsichord.

channels.name: Shoegaze Revival
channels.profile: You're dipping into shoegaze with an open ear, drawn to both its lush, dreamy textures and its more driving, rhythmic energy. You seem to enjoy variety but may favor songs that avoid the genre's harshest, most abrasive peaks.

currentCard.reason: An intense, building wall of sound with a driving energy that contrasts with your calmer likes, exploring a more aggressive edge of shoegaze.

queue.reason: A noisy yet delicate wall of guitars with a sweeping melody, pushing energy higher while retaining the dreamy haze — venturing toward the denser, more abrasive side of the genre.
Ethereal, swirling guitar work and Elizabeth Fraser's soaring, wordless vocals — exploring a more abstract, ambient-adjacent corner of the dream-pop/shoegaze border.

While you've heard Vapour Trail (Video), this is the original studio version — driving, energetic shoegaze with bright guitar swirls and a more upbeat rhythm, exploring the higher-energy end of the spectrum.

channels.notes:
Rock → Shoegaze Revival
Known for dreamy, ethereal soundscapes and introspective lyrics, shoegaze originally peaked in the early 90s and was declared dead for decades. It's back, driven by a younger generation discovering My Bloody Valentine and immediately wanting to make their own version.

channels.name: Ustad Mohammed Omar
channels.profile: Your taste so far leans toward introspective, traditional acoustic textures from the Hindustani classical world, especially the rabab. You seem to appreciate meditative, slow-burning pieces but also enjoy rhythmic vitality when kept within acoustic boundaries.

currentCard.reason: A lighter, melodious raga with a folk-inflected feel, played on rubab with gentle ornamentation and acoustic warmth.

queue.reason: A lighter, folk-influenced raga on rubab, offering a more melodic and accessible side of the same tradition.
A soothing raga with a gentle lilt, perfect for your preference for calm, unadorned acoustic textures.
A lesser-known rubab raga with a gentle, introspective mood at a moderately higher energy level, exploring a new part of the space with minimal mainstream exposure.

channels.name: Verve Records
channels.profile: You're drawn to classic Verve jazz with acoustic warmth and timeless melodies. Your taste swings between intimate ballads and energetic swing, favoring accessible yet sophisticated sounds from the 1950s–60s.

currentCard.reason: A jazz standard with warm acoustic swing that lands in the accessible mainstream region of Verve's catalog.

queue.reason: Soul-jazz with a groovy Hammond organ—dense, funky, and club-oriented but still acoustic.

track.name: What Is This Thing Called Love? [Verve Jazz Masters 28 vers.]

queue.reason: Bebop intensity with fast alto saxophone lines—high energy, cult historical significance.

track.name: Turn Out the Stars: The Final Village Vanguard Recordings, June 1980

queue.reason: Intimate, sparse piano trio with subtle harmonic exploration—a calm, introspective acoustic corner with cult prestige.
